vocab.meaning: sự thận trọng trong chi tiêu tài chính
vocab.example: I exercise fiscal prudence when it comes to footwear, ensuring that I don't overspend on trendy items that will soon go out of style.

vocab.meaning: một khoản đầu tư lớn (đắt tiền)
vocab.example: Buying a pair of high-quality leather boots is a hefty investment, but they are far more cost-effective than cheaper alternatives in the long run.

vocab.meaning: sự trung thành với thương hiệu
vocab.example: My brand loyalty to certain athletic companies often leads me to pay a bit more for their latest running models.

vocab.meaning: một khoản tiền không đáng kể
vocab.example: To be honest, I spend a negligible amount on shoes because I prefer a minimalist lifestyle with only two or three essential pairs.
I refuse to pay exorbitant prices for designer labels just for the sake of status.
The annual replacement cost of my gym shoes is relatively high because I wear them out quite quickly with daily exercise.
Being quality-conscious, I am willing to save up for several months to afford a pair that offers superior arch support.

vocab.meaning: chi phí không chính đáng/hợp lý
vocab.example: For a student like me, spending over $200 on casual footwear would be an unjustified expense.

vocab.meaning: đợt xả hàng theo mùa (giảm giá)
vocab.example: I typically limit my shopping to seasonal clearance events to get the best value for my money.

vocab.meaning: những hạn chế về ngân sách
vocab.example: Due to strict budgetary constraints, I have to be very selective about which shoes I purchase each year.
